topic.what: Systematic characterization of keystroke dynamics for human-vs-machine discrimination in HID injection detection
topic.how: Lightweight timing-based models (RF/SVM/CNN/LSTM) operating on Hold Time and Flight Time features, excluding key content
topic.why_matters: USB HID emulation attacks bypass traditional defenses; existing keystroke dynamics work focuses on user authentication rather than injection detection; this enables privacy-preserving detection without user profiling or invasive instrumentation

characteristics.what_it_measures: ROC-AUC for distinguishing human from synthetic keystrokes
characteristics.vs_baseline: Single-generator training yields near-perfect in-distribution separation
characteristics.evidence: Figure 6: RF detector at 70 keystrokes achieves ROC-AUC>0.9 for most generators; UC3 achieves ROC-AUC>0.9 across diverse attacker models
characteristics.context: Mixed-generator training UC3 with NS-Hist 70%, Cond-Bin 20%, Unc.WGAN-GP 7%, Average 3%

characteristics.what_itasures: Input sequence length balancing detection timeliness and reliability
characteristics.vs_baseline: Performance saturates beyond 70-100 keystrokes with marginal gains
characteristics.evidence: Figures 8-9 show rapid performance increase 10-70 keystrokes, stabilization 70-100, marginal gains beyond
characteristics.context: Early detection with 70 keystrokes enables effective attack interception before command completion

characteristics.what_it_measures: Total time from keystroke observation to classification decision
characteristics.vs_baseline: Stable across input sizes from 10 to 200 keystrokes
characteristics.evidence: Figure 10a: Total latency ~89-94ms for RF detector; preprocessing latency nearly constant
characteristics.context: Supports continuous background deployment without usability impact

characteristics.evidence: Figure 10b: CPU process ~5.118-5.125% normalized by 20 threads
characteristics.context: Lightweight deployment suitable for continuous monitoring

characteristics.vs_baseline: Decreases with longer input sequences due to data-driven tree simplification
characteristics.evidence: Figure 10b: RAM loading 420-520MB; longer sequences allow simpler trees reducing memory
characteristics.context: Fixed tree count (n_estimators=300) but complexity is data-driven not strictly input-dependent

characteristics.what_itasures: Detector robustness across structurally distinct attacker families
characteristics.vs_baseline: Statistical-trained detectors generalize to PRNG but not to Cond-Bin/Emp-Pair
characteristics.evidence: Figure 6c,d: NS-Hist trained detectors achieve ROC-AUC>0.9 on Statistical and adaptive datasets; Naive-trained poor cross-family
characteristics.context: Training coverage of structurally distinct families determines generalization more than model complexity

requires: Offline human keystroke data for training

novelty: First systematic characterization of keystroke dynamics for human-vs-machine discrimination independent of user identity
Identification that detection robustness depends on coverage of structurally distinct attacker families rather than model complexity
Quantification of detection timeliness vs reliability trade-off with practical operating points
Demonstration that GAN-based attackers do not monotonically improve evasion capability
open_problems: Optimal training composition for unknown attacker profiles
Real-time adaptive detector updating against evolving attacks
Performance on mobile and virtual keyboards
